reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — driven headless-Chrome (no-GPU) end-to-end across all 5 pages, desktop+mobile+light/dark screenshots read

rubric.evidence: Source calls a REAL WebLLM path: worker.js imports createEngine from lib/webllm.js and streams via engine.chat.completions.create({stream:true}); the page gates on webGPUAdapterAvailable() before load. Verified the MLC id Qwen3-1.7B-q4f16_1-MLC EXISTS in @mlc-ai/web-llm 0.2.84 prebuiltAppConfig (esm.run serves 0.2.84) — vram_required_MB 2036, context 4096. In the no-GPU headless runner every page reached the honest 'unsupported / needs-WebGPU (no GPU adapter here)' state — NEVER a faked token.
rubric.notes: Real streamed token output on a GPU device was not observable in this no-GPU sandbox by design (WebLLM is WebGPU-only, no WASM fallback). The load path and MLC id are verified; a GPU-device run to observe live decode + reasoning stream is the open item.

rubric.evidence: Expected shape is a hybrid reasoning stream: a <think>…</think> (or reasoning_content) reasoning segment then an answer. worker.js splits the two channels (splitThink + reasoning_content handling) with partial-tag holdback, and posts tagged reasoning/answer deltas. The UI renders reasoning into a collapsible <details class=think> trace and the answer into the bubble; see-inside shows reasoning-tokens vs answer-tokens vs tok/s. Practical page runs JSON.parse() on the answer to prove machine-readable output. Wild page runs the SAME prompt /no_think vs /think side by side.
rubric.notes: Semantic sanity of the reasoning/answer split is verified in code and the wire panel; observing the exact <think> content requires a GPU device (openQuestion).

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (Qwen/Qwen3-1.7B linked to card), declares-task (text-generation), declares-backend (WebGPU), declares-quantisation (q4f16_1), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance (read in the desktop dark screenshot) lists model, MLC build Qwen3-1.7B-q4f16_1-MLC, runtime WebLLM/MLC (not Transformers.js), params 1.7B q4f16_1, thinking control /think·/no_think, chat template Qwen ChatML with real system role, backend WebGPU-only, download ~1.1 GB, context 4096, license Apache-2.0.
rubric.notes: Honest, complete disclosure; matches exactly what worker.js loads (MODEL_ID Qwen3-1.7B-q4f16_1-MLC via createEngine).

rubric.evidence: Every page uses the shared createModelLoader (no bespoke Load button). WebGPU gate fires first: on the no-GPU runner the loader lands in 'unsupported' with the honest needs-WebGPU message and NO action button, and the fallback panel explains how to enable WebGPU + the ~1.1 GB cost — no silent download. Per-model 'Clear cached model' control is provided by the shared loader once ready.
rubric.notes: On a GPU device the state matrix (absent→Download, current→auto-init no button, partial→Re-download) is driven by the same shared loader as the other WebLLM pages; the needs-WebGPU honest gate is what this environment can prove.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present on every page), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader), webgpu-capability-honest (real adapter probe → labelled unsupported). Driven runs: 0 uncaught JS errors, 0 console errors, 0 failed network requests on all 5 pages at desktop AND mobile. Generation failures set status .err with the message; Stop calls engine.interruptGenerate().
rubric.notes: Honest needs-WebGPU rendered on all pages. Offline-cached / partial-evicted state drives need a GPU device with a warm cache (openQuestion).

rubric.evidence: Main: thinking-mode checkbox (updates the /think·/no_think hint), system-prompt textarea, temp/top_p/max-tokens ranges with live value bind, message textarea, Send/Stop/Clear. Basics: thinking toggle + example chips + Ask. Practical: reason-first checkbox + example chips + schema input + Extract, with live JSON.parse validity. Wild: prompt textarea + example chips + 'Compare both modes'. Multi-model: two shared loaders + thinking toggle + KB textarea + question. Every control is a real labelled element; chips/toggles ≥ ~2.2rem tap target.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced via rendered screenshots; a full keyboard-only traversal (tab order + visible focus on every control across all 5 pages, Enter-to-send/Shift+Enter) on a GPU device is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: Before a run every answer/reasoning area shows only a muted placeholder; on the no-GPU runner the loader shows the honest unsupported state and NO tokens are ever synthesised. There is no canned reply path anywhere — the answer only ever comes from engine.chat.completions.create.
rubric.notes: Verified across all 5 pages: no pre-baked result, and the WebGPU gate prevents any output when the model genuinely can't run.

rubric.evidence:
conformance: images-have-alt (no content ), buttons-have-names (all buttons have text). role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; chat log + answer areas aria-live=polite; textareas have aria-label; example chips grouped with role=group aria-label; thinking-trace uses a native
/ disclosure (keyboard-operable, reduced-motion respected on the caret/spinner/marker).
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the streaming reasoning trace + answer on a GPU device would further validate.

rubric.evidence: Driven matrix: documentElement.scrollWidth - clientWidth == 0 (no horizontal overflow) at 1280x800 AND 360x740 (DPR3, touch) on all 5 pages. Screenshots read: qwen3 main (desktop dark) — at-a-glance table wraps, lede clean; wild (mobile) — the two-column versus grid stacks to one column, heading wraps, needs-WebGPU panel legible. min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere on bubbles/flex items; grids use auto-fit minmax(min(100%,Npx),1fr).
rubric.notes: Support recorded needs-review→ok pending the responsive-check seed; driven no-overflow pass across desktop+mobile is strong evidence. A real touch device pass of the sliders/details is a nicety.

rubric.evidence: All inference AND the reasoning/answer stream split run in a dedicated module Web Worker (worker.js, new Worker(url,{type:'module'})) so decode + tag-splitting never block the main thread; tokens post incrementally via postMessage and the UI only appends text. conformance worker-compliance=source-pass, inference-off-main-thread=pass. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture).
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement while streaming on a GPU device would confirm; the off-thread worker + incremental streaming is the key mitigation, matching the reference WebLLM pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: stream LLM tokens incrementally without blocking main thread INP web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy work off the main thread and yield/stream incrementally so it never becomes a long task that spikes INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all generation and the reasoning/answer channel split run in models/qwen3-webllm/worker.js; the worker posts small tagged deltas the main thread just appends. No synchronous main-thread loop over tokens.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance worker-compliance + inference-off-main-thread pass; driven runs stayed responsive with 0 console errors on all 5 pages

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel form controls no horizontal overflow narrow mobile
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + gap + intrinsic sizing and set min-inline-size:0 on flex items with long unbreakable content; use grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,Npx),1fr) so columns stack instead of overflowing.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — composer/controls/chips use flex-wrap+gap; chat bubbles + composer textarea set min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere; the wild 'versus' grid and multi-model loader/result grids use auto-fit minmax(min(100%,Npx),1fr); glance cells overflow-wrap:anywhere.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: scrollWidth-clientWidth==0 at 360x740 and 1280x800 on all 5 pages; wild-mobile screenshot shows the versus grid stacked with no overflow

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible collapsible details disclosure reasoning trace live region status
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region, label every control, prefer native disclosure semantics, and respect prefers-reduced-motion.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status; aria-live=polite chat log + answer; aria-label on textareas; role=group on chip sets; the reasoning trace is a native <details>/<summary> (keyboard-operable); caret/spinner/marker animations are disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region=pass, buttons-have-names=pass; DOM inspection + rendered screenshots

guidanceConsulted.query: textarea that grows to fit content field-sizing
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use field-sizing:content with min/max block-size so composer textareas grow with input instead of scrolling.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (shared CHAT_CSS) — .composer textarea and .sysbox textarea use field-sizing:content with min/max block-size caps, so the message/system inputs grow with content on both viewports.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: CHAT_CSS in models/qwen3-webllm/webllm-chat.js; no overflow observed on mobile

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (invariant 6); every page links the shared stylesheet and crumbs back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs present on all 5 pages (read in screenshots)

openQuestions: On a real WebGPU device: observe the live <think> reasoning stream vs answer stream, confirm the partial-tag holdback never shows a stray '</thi' fragment, and measure reasoning-tokens vs answer-tokens + tok/s in the see-inside panel.
Verify WebLLM 0.2.84 surfaces Qwen3 reasoning as delta.reasoning_content vs inline <think> tags on this specific MLC build, so the worker's dual-path splitter is exercised on the real output.
Keyboard-only traversal + visible focus across all 5 pages on a GPU device (Enter-to-send/Shift+Enter, the thinking checkbox, the details disclosure).
Drive the warm-cache auto-init (no button) + partial/evicted + offline-cached states on a GPU device with the ~1.1 GB model cached.

followUpGoals.goal: GPU-device verification pass for qwen3-webllm: capture the live reasoning trace, reasoning-vs-answer token counts, and the wild page's /think vs /no_think accuracy+latency contrast as retained evidence.
Add a 'thinking budget' see-inside control (cap reasoning tokens) to show the accuracy/latency trade-off Qwen3's thinking mode exposes.
Add a Qwen3 tool-calling / structured-output demo that reasons about which tool to call before emitting the call.

summary: qwen3-webllm is a strong, honest, genuinely-distinct demo: the catalogue's first HYBRID reasoning model, running Qwen3-1.7B-q4f16_1-MLC (verified present in @mlc-ai/web-llm 0.2.84 prebuiltAppConfig) on WebLLM/WebGPU. Its distinguishing feature is real: a /think vs /no_think toggle and a worker that separates the streamed <think> reasoning trace from the answer, surfaced as a collapsible trace + reasoning-vs-answer token counts, with a wild page that runs the same prompt both ways side by side. Driven end-to-end headless (no-GPU): honest needs-WebGPU state on every page (no faked tokens), 0 JS/console errors, 0 failed requests, and no horizontal overflow at 1280x800 or 360x740 across all 5 pages (main + basics/practical/wild/multi-model RAG), light + dark both legible. Real streamed decode is verified by source + MLC-id existence; observing live tokens needs a GPU device (the high-priority follow-up). Revision 1.
